summary: Recent updates to the testing framework for Firefox have enhanced the reliability and coverage of key media features. Notably, the **GTest framework now includes tests for HEVC (High Efficiency Video Coding) support**, ensuring that the browser consistently handles video formats properly while checking for any potential issues when certain file extensions are missing. Additionally, tests have also been re-enabled for hardware-accelerated H.264 support on MacOS, which is important for improving video playback performance. These changes collectively bolster the robustness of Firefox’s media capabilities, making video streaming and playback more reliable across platforms.
